title: Personal Information Technology — Aberdeen Practice Launch
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: Program-launch description of Aberdeen Group's Personal Information Technology practice, established by Peter Kastner in 2001. Articulates the nine market paradoxes driving the practice (PC commoditization, digital-device convergence, home-network emergence, 3G wireless, digital TV transition, in-home services needs), catalogs service offerings, technology segments, strategic market questions, key market trends, and the supplier coverage list (Microsoft, Intel, AMD, Compaq, Dell, HP, IBM, Sony, Toshiba, Apple, Gateway, Micron, AOL, Epson, Lexmark, Canon, Nvidia, Cirrus Logic).
importance_rationale: Foundational documentation of Aberdeen's Personal IT practice — establishes the analytical framework under which subsequent DCT, weekly PC Deals, and lab-report work is produced.
relevance_rationale: Primary-source description of a research program still referenced in IT industry-analyst histories; relevant for understanding Kastner's 2001-2005 output.
prescience_rationale: Prescient framing — the practice's nine drivers (PC commoditization, home networks, digital TV, 3G wireless, digital photography, multi-PC households, digital appliances, MP3-replacing-cassette, broadband last-mile) map closely to the 2002-2008 consumer-tech reality.
